title: The ferry at sunrise in Istanbul
summary: A short description of an early morning ferry ride across a city's water.
topic: The ferry at sunrise in Istanbul
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a ferry, bus, train, or tram ride at a specific time of day in another city. Describe the light, the people, and what the city looked like. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: On my last morning in Istanbul, I caught the six-fifteen ferry from…
The sky was still half dark, and the water was…
An old man next to me was already drinking…
sampleText: On my last morning in Istanbul, I caught the six-fifteen ferry from Karaköy to Kadıköy. The sky was still half dark when I bought my token, and the water was the colour of cold tea. I climbed up to the open deck because I wanted to see the city change colour from the middle of the Bosphorus. Only about twenty other passengers were on board, mostly commuters in dark coats. An old man next to me was already drinking small glass of black tea from the man with the tray. While the ferry was pulling away from the pier, the first orange light hit the Galata Tower and turned its top into a small gold candle. Seagulls flew very close to the upper deck, almost touching my hat. A young woman across from me was reading a thick novel in Turkish without ever looking up. We passed two other ferries going the opposite way, and each one sounded its horn once, like a slow polite greeting. Twenty-two minutes later, the engine slowed and the ferry slid against the Kadıköy pier. I stepped off into a city that had become bright while I was crossing it. I have caught early ferries in three other cities since, but never one that felt like that.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for parallel scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'was pulling away', 'was drinking', 'was reading' — nhiều past continuous song song dựng cảnh trên phà tại một khoảnh khắc quá khứ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Was pulling away', 'was drinking', 'was reading' — multiple past continuous verbs in parallel set the scene on the ferry at a single past moment.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for a completed change
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'a city that had become bright while I was crossing it' — past perfect ('had become') chỉ sự thay đổi đã hoàn thành trước thời điểm tôi xuống bến.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'A city that had become bright while I was crossing it' — past perfect ('had become') marks a change completed before the moment I disembarked.

sampleTranslationVi: Vào sáng cuối cùng của tôi ở Istanbul, tôi đón chuyến phà sáu giờ mười lăm từ Karaköy đến Kadıköy. Bầu trời vẫn còn nửa tối khi tôi mua xèng đi phà, và nước mang màu trà nguội. Tôi trèo lên boong mở vì tôi muốn thấy thành phố đổi màu từ giữa eo Bosphorus. Chỉ khoảng hai mươi hành khách khác trên tàu, chủ yếu là người đi làm trong áo khoác sẫm màu. Một ông cụ bên cạnh tôi đã đang uống một cốc nhỏ trà đen từ người đàn ông cầm khay. Trong khi chiếc phà đang rời khỏi cầu cảng, tia sáng cam đầu tiên chiếu vào tháp Galata và biến đỉnh tháp thành một ngọn nến vàng nhỏ. Hải âu bay rất gần boong trên, gần như chạm vào mũ tôi. Một phụ nữ trẻ đối diện tôi đang đọc một cuốn tiểu thuyết dày bằng tiếng Thổ mà không ngẩng lên một lần nào. Chúng tôi đi ngang hai chiếc phà khác đi ngược chiều, và mỗi chiếc bóp còi một lần, như một lời chào chậm rãi lịch sự. Hai mươi hai phút sau, động cơ chậm lại và chiếc phà trượt áp vào bến Kadıköy. Tôi bước xuống một thành phố đã trở nên sáng trong khi tôi đang băng qua nó. Tôi đã đón phà sáng sớm ở ba thành phố khác kể từ đó, nhưng chưa bao giờ có chuyến nào cảm thấy giống như chuyến ấy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple opening.
options: On my last morning in Istanbul, I caught the six-fifteen ferry from Karaköy to Kadıköy.
On my last morning in Istanbul, I catch the six-fifteen ferry from Karaköy to Kadıköy.
On my last morning in Istanbul, I am catching the six-fifteen ferry from Karaköy to Kadıköy.
On my last morning in Istanbul, I have caught the six-fifteen ferry from Karaköy to Kadıköy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While the ferry was pulling away from the pier, the first orange light hit the Galata Tower.
While the ferry pulled away from the pier, the first orange light was hit the Galata Tower.
While the ferry is pulling away from the pier, the first orange light hit the Galata Tower.
While the ferry pulling away from the pier, the first orange light hit the Galata Tower.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous for background; past simple 'hit' for the inserted action.

options: a person who travels regularly between home and work
a tourist on a short trip
a person selling tickets
the captain of a boat

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural past continuous sentence.
options: An old man next to me was already drinking a small glass of black tea.
An old man next to me already drinking a small glass of black tea.
An old man next to me already drink a small glass of black tea.
An old man next to me is already drinking a small glass of black tea.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was drinking' for an ongoing past action; 'already' before main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple sentence about flying birds.
options: Seagulls flew very close to the upper deck.
Seagulls flied very close to the upper deck.
Seagulls flying very close to the upper deck.
Seagulls have flew very close to the upper deck.
the land along the edge of the sea or a river
the middle of the water
the top of a building
a small island
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Shore' = the land bordering a body of water.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'past + object'.
options: We passed two other ferries going the opposite way.
We past two other ferries going the opposite way.
We passed two other ferries gone the opposite way.
We are passed two other ferries going the opposite way.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple verb 'passed' (not the noun/adjective 'past'). Reduced relative 'going' for movement.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past simple sentence with 'sound (one's) horn'.
options: Each one sounded its horn once.
Each one sound its horn once.
Each one is sounded its horn once.
Each one has sound its horn once.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past perfect relative clause.
options: a city that had become bright while I was crossing it
a city that has become bright while I was crossing it
a city that was become bright while I was crossing it
a city that had became bright while I was crossing it
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had become' for a change completed before the past moment; past participle 'become' (same as base).

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct present perfect sentence about repeated experience.
options: I have caught early ferries in three other cities since.
I caught early ferries in three other cities since.
I am caught early ferries in three other cities since.
I had caught early ferries in three other cities since.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect 'have caught' for experiences from past to now.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural closing line.
options: Never one that felt like that.
Never one that feel like that.
Never one that felt like that one.
Never one which felt like as that.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'felt'; 'like that' is the natural ending phrase.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do certain rides — a particular ferry, a particular bus at a particular hour — stay in the memory longer than expensive trips we plan for months?
